summary.intent: Implement Matrix OIDC authentication (Issue #711) to support homeservers using Matrix Authentication Service (MAS/MSC3861), since matrix.org and others have moved away from password auth.
summary.outcome: Full OIDC two-phase browser flow implemented (oauth_start/oauth_complete RPC methods, callback handler integration, session persistence, token refresh), with UI for all three modals and onboarding. Several post-merge bugs fixed including: wrong callback path, redirect_uri scheme normalization for MAS, status detection for OIDC sessions, ownership mode not passed through, and cross-signing bootstrap without password.

repo: matrix-sdk Client.user_id().is_some() works for both matrix_auth and oauth sessions; matrix_auth().logged_in() returns false for OIDC sessions
MAS (matrix.org OIDC) rejects https://localhost redirect_uri — requires http://localhost per RFC 8252 loopback rule, but client_uri must be a real HTTPS project URL (not localhost)

ChannelPlugin trait methods must be overridden in impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin, not as inherent methods on MatrixPlugin — gateway dispatches through dyn ChannelPlugin and only sees trait methods
The OAuth callback route is /auth/callback, not /api/oauth/callback
matrix_sdk::store::RoomLoadSettings is re-exported from matrix_sdk (not just matrix_sdk_base) and can be used without adding matrix-sdk-base as a direct dep
Raw::new(val: &T) returns serde_json::Result<Self> — must be unwrapped; Localized::new requires explicit Vec<(LanguageTag, T)> type annotation for empty vecs
Cross-signing bootstrap for OIDC uses bootstrap_cross_signing_if_needed(None) — MAS handles auth without password; password-based UIAA path errors with 'password is required'
The ownership_mode and other config fields were not passed through oidc_start RPC — gateway built a hardcoded minimal config ignoring form fields
channels.status response uses ch.status === 'connected', not ch.connected

code.finding: matrix_auth().logged_in() check for connection status — must be replaced with client.user_id().is_some() to detect OIDC sessions
oidc_start gateway handler built hardcoded minimal config, discarding ownership_mode and all other form fields — needed to forward full config object from JS params
Safari browser paint bug: when button text changes (saving state), old text pixels remain composited. Fix: key the <button> element on the state value to force DOM node recreation instead of patch
transition: opacity 0.15s on .provider-btn combined with opacity: 0.4 on :disabled causes ghost text when Preact patches text node simultaneously with disabled state change

workflow: Running cargo check/clippy on individual crates (-p moltis-matrix) is faster than workspace-wide checks during iteration
Greptile automated review caught real bugs: wrong response field (res.result vs res.payload), missing file permissions (0o600), and unused oidc_issuer config field
greploop pattern: submit PR → fix comments → push → trigger re-review → repeat until 0 comments works well for iterative PR review
Preact HTM template buttons with dynamic text need key=${state} to force element recreation in Safari — text node patching doesn't trigger repaint

friction: Multiple roundtrips to fix MAS redirect_uri rejection: first tried http:// normalization (worked for registration but broke callback), then reverted (broke registration again), then restored normalization — needed real MAS test environment from the start
oidc_start/oidc_complete implemented as inherent MatrixPlugin methods but gateway dispatches through dyn ChannelPlugin trait — silently returned 'OIDC not supported' default error until discovered
Safari ghost button text bug took 7+ iterations to diagnose and fix (opacity transition → isolation: isolate → span wrapper → keyed span → keyed button)
Greptile re-flagged already-fixed comments on new commits because it associates old review comments with new commit line numbers
normalize_redirect_uri was added, removed, and re-added across commits due to uncertainty about MAS behavior without a test environment
oidc_issuer config field was designed but matrix-sdk 0.16 has no API to override the issuer URL — had to remove it after Greptile flagged it as unused
open_items: E2E tests (channels-matrix.spec.js) mock channels.oauth_start but cannot test full OIDC flow against real MAS — integration tests against a MAS instance would catch redirect_uri/client_uri policy issues earlier
Re-authenticate button in EditMatrixModal (plan step 7) was not fully implemented — plan called for a button to trigger new OIDC flow for existing channels
Ghost button fix applied only to onboarding-view.js buttons — page-channels.js modal buttons may have same Safari paint issue and were partially fixed but should be audited
Duplicate Matrix account guard added but account_id is randomly generated — same user could still accumulate multiple OIDC sessions if they repeatedly go through the flow without removing old ones
oidc_issuer override removed as unused — if matrix-sdk adds API to override issuer URL in a future version, this feature should be re-added

Cross-signing bootstrap for OIDC moltis_owned mode: ensure_moltis_owned_encryption_oidc added but untested end-to-end for whether MAS actually accepts bootstrap_cross_signing_if_needed(None) without additional OAuth interaction
